John Samberg, of Wolf, Rifkin, Shapiro, Schulman & Rabkin, LLP, Tel. 775.853.6787, for plaintiff/cross-defendant and opposing party Blue Tee Corp.;
Katrina Telfer Saleen, of Broderick Law Firm, Tel. 650.857.9000, for defendant and moving party Diversified Product Industries Ltd.;
David McPherson, of Watt, Tieder, Hoffar & Fitzgerald, LLP, Tel. 949.852.6700, for defendants Bernard Impregilo Healy Joint Venture, Barnard Construction Company, Inc., appearing via CourtCall.
No other appearances.

RULING: DEFENDANT DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES LTD. Motion For Orders Quashing Deposition Subpoenas For Personal Appearance And Production Of Documents And Things Directed To Access Solar, Inc. And Cabazon Development Corp. are GRANTED, without prejudice to future discovery. The fact that (1) Jack C. Pryor is listed on the CSLB website as the licensed contractor for DPI, Access, and Cabazon; (2) Access' website content references DPI; (3) DPI, Access, and Cabaon share an insurance policy; (4) a Work Order Estimate identifies Access for the work site and DPI as the billing entity; and (5) DPI was denied credit for a solar lift do not show good cause for the subpoenas. Information as to whether "DPI's business was intentionally slowed in order for its resources to be used to capitalize Access" (Opp, 6:8-9) can be obtained through discovery directly from DPI. The requests for sanctions is denied. ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
